title: Clarify remote client means Mac and Windows
body:
Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@n1hility could you verify for Windows.  Do we use Podman-remote with Windows, or is it actually a full fledge podman client that WSL is showing?
On WSL 2 it is a Linux client.  I just want to make sure that readers don't confuse the two.

I'm probably being badly affected by PTO brain today, but why the "(excluding WSL2)" bit?  I thought Podman on windows required WSL2?  Unless I'm misunderstanding, you're saying "this option doesn't work on Windows (excluding Windows)".  But perhaps it's time for the first cup of tea.

@TomSweeneyRedHat If you grab the podman exe for Windows, it is remote and is podman-remote. If you run within WLS2 you are running the Linux Podman directly, through WSL magic. So you can run it in either mode. There is a PR to add qemu based images just like Podman on a Mac works.

Would it be clearer to say “(unless within machine ssh or WSL2 prompt)”? 

I mainly mean the explanation applies to both Mac and Windows. 

Edit since forgot to answer question:
@rhatdan that's correct on Windows its podman remote when they are in PowerShell or execing from Windows tools, and when they either do podman machine ssh or they pull up a WSL prompt to enter the podman distro it is full podman on Linux
It is getting pretty long at that point.  I think what we have is enough.  Most users will understand that podman on a MAC will default to podman-remote.
